title: Pleurogramme minor
intro: Pleurogramme minor is a species of fern, specifically a type of liverwort, that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleurogramme minor include compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pleurogramme minor is often used to make preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat infections, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Pleurogramme minor is classified as a species of liverwort, specifically within the family Pleurogrammaceae. It belongs to the phylum Marchantiophyta, which encompasses all liverworts. The class Marchantiopsida is also part of its classification, and it is further grouped under the subclass Marchantiidae. The order Marchantiales is another classification level for this species. This classification helps in understanding its relationships with other liverwort species.
